summary: Backend testing for role management (Teams + Reports + RBAC scoping) complete. 29/29 tests passed against public URL. Covered Teams CRUD, member assign/remove/leader promotion, /my-team RBAC, /reports/me | /reports/team | /reports/global scoping, /admin/users and /admin/analytics team scoping, /leaderboard?team_id filter, team_id backfill for seeded users, and JWT bearer auth.

minor.issue: The team's own leader_id/leader is set correctly, but /api/teams response includes the leader user only via `leader` object; consider adding a `members` preview or last_active timestamps for the Team Leader dashboard convenience.
Per-member breakdown does N+1 count_documents queries (5 per member). Fine for current scale but should be aggregated with $group for larger teams.
Response spec says teams sorted by xp — currently returned in Mongo insertion order. Not enforced by tests but doc says sorted.

action_items: Consider sorting `teams` array in /api/reports/global by xp desc as documented.
Consider aggregating per-member counts in /api/reports/team to reduce N+1 DB round-trips.
critical_code_review_comments: RBAC boundaries are correctly enforced by require_role at every teams/reports endpoint — verified via 403 tests for leader/member on super_admin-only routes and 403 for member on team_leader routes.
seed_admin_and_indexes correctly upserts teams (Command/Alpha/Bravo/Delta) and backfills team_id for pre-existing demo users — Alpha now shows member_count >= 2 with leader@spartans.com as leader.
Team rename correctly propagates the new team name to all members via update_many on team_id — good data-consistency handling.
Team delete correctly unassigns all members (team_id=None, team=None) and demotes the team_leader — good cleanup.
assign_to_team with is_leader=true correctly demotes the previous leader and updates teams.leader_id atomically.
/admin/analytics correctly returns scope='team' vs scope='global' based on role — clean scoping pattern.
/api/leaderboard?team_id=<id> filters by team members without requiring elevated role, allowing transparency while preserving privacy (only user rows for that team returned).
MINOR: N+1 count_documents in /reports/team members loop and /reports/global teams loop — pre-emptive perf note.
MINOR: /reports/global does not sort teams by xp despite spec wording; consider `per_team.sort(key=lambda t: t['xp'], reverse=True)`.
seed_data_creation: Test-only teams created with TEST_Titans_* prefix and cleaned up in module teardown. Test users registered with TEST_assign_* email prefix (not deleted — no user-delete endpoint exposed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 15 requested role-management backend flows verified. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_teams_and_reports.py uses function-scoped auth fixtures to avoid session pollution across role-switching tests. Reuse the same _login helper pattern when adding new tests. If pytest.ini has -n auto (xdist), pass `-o addopts=''` to run serially — needed because created_test_team fixture is shared across the module.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in role management implementation.
